title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
chief_complaint: I have irregular periods since I was a teenager - I only get 4-5 periods a year. I've gained 30 pounds, I have acne on my face and back, and I have excess hair growth on my chin and upper lip. I've been trying to get pregnant for 1 year with no success.

physical_exam.Skin: Moderate acne on face and back. Hirsutism: Ferriman-Gallwey score 18 (moderate-severe - terminal hair on upper lip, chin, chest, lower abdomen, inner thighs). Male-pattern alopecia (scalp).
physical_exam.Pelvic: Normal uterus. Bilateral ovaries slightly enlarged (3-4cm on bimanual - normal <3cm).

hidden_data.pelvic_us: Bilateral enlarged ovaries (Right 12mL, Left 10mL). Multiple peripheral follicles (string of pearls - >12 follicles 2-9mm per ovary). No dominant follicle. Endometrial thickness 12mm (thickened - unopposed estrogen from anovulation).
hidden_data.hormones: LH 18 (elevated), FSH 5 (normal), LH:FSH ratio >3:1 (classic PCOS). Testosterone 85 ng/dL (elevated - normal <50). Free testosterone elevated. DHEAS 350 (mildly elevated - adrenal component). AMH 10 ng/mL (elevated - increased follicular mass). Estradiol 65 (normal follicular). Prolactin normal. TSH 2.5 (normal). 17-OHP normal (rules out late-onset CAH).
hidden_data.metabolic: Fasting glucose 105 (impaired fasting glucose). 2h OGTT 155 (impaired glucose tolerance). HbA1c 5.9%. Fasting insulin 25 (elevated - insulin resistance). HOMA-IR 5.5 (severe insulin resistance). Lipid panel: LDL 140, HDL 32, TG 220.
hidden_data.endometrial_biopsy: Endometrial biopsy (performed for thickened lining + prolonged unopposed estrogen): Proliferative endometrium. No hyperplasia or atypia.

correct_diagnosis: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) - Rotterdam Criteria (Oligo-ovulation + Hyperandrogenism + Polycystic Ovaries) with Insulin Resistance, Infertility, and Hirsutism
key_learning_points: PCOS = most common endocrine disorder in reproductive-age women (5-10%). Etiology: Insulin resistance → hyperinsulinemia → increased ovarian androgen production + decreased SHBG → hyperandrogenism. LH hypersecretion from GnRH pulse generator dysfunction

Rotterdam criteria (2/3 required): 1) Oligo/anovulation (<8 cycles/year or cycles >35 days). 2) Clinical or biochemical hyperandrogenism (hirsutism, acne, alopecia, elevated testosterone). 3) Polycystic ovaries on ultrasound (>12 follicles 2-9mm or ovarian volume >10mL). Rule out other causes: CAH (17-OHP), Cushing (cortisol), Thyroid dysfunction (TSH), Hyperprolactinemia (prolactin), Androgen-secreting tumor (rapid onset, severe virilization)

Metabolic complications: Insulin resistance (50-70%), Impaired glucose tolerance (30-40% by age 40), T2DM (10% by age 40 - 5-10× increased risk), Dyslipidemia, Obesity (50%). Screen with 2h OGTT (not just fasting glucose), lipid panel, HbA1c q1-2 years

Long-term risks: Endometrial hyperplasia/cancer (unopposed estrogen from chronic anovulation → 3× risk). Ensure regular withdrawal bleeds (OCPs or cyclic Progestins q1-3 months). Cardiovascular disease (metabolic syndrome). OSA (5-10× risk). Depression and anxiety (increased risk)

Treatment: 1) LIFESTYLE (cornerstone): Weight loss 5-10% (restores ovulation in 60%, improves insulin resistance, reduces androgens). Exercise 150min/week. Mediterranean diet. 2) COMBINED HORMONAL CONTRACEPTIVES (first-line for menstrual irregularity + hyperandrogenism): OCPs (ethinyl estradiol + progestin) - suppress LH, increase SHBG (decreases free testosterone), regulate cycles, protect endometrium, improve acne/hirsutism. 3) ANTI-ANDROGENS: Spironolactone 50-200mg/day (androgen receptor blocker - monitor K+, teratogenic - avoid pregnancy). Finasteride (5-alpha reductase inhibitor - teratogenic). Eflornithine cream (topical for facial hair). Laser hair removal. 4) METFORMIN 500-2,000mg/day: Improves insulin resistance, restores ovulation (30-50%), reduces androgens. First-line if metabolic abnormalities present. 5) FERTILITY: Letrozole 2.5-7.5mg days 3-7 (first-line ovulation induction - superior to Clomiphene in PCOS). Clomiphene citrate (second-line). Gonadotropins (low-dose step-up protocol - high risk of OHSS and multiple pregnancy). IVF if fails. Laparoscopic ovarian drilling (second-line - reduces androgen-producing stroma, restores ovulation in 50%)
